factors.summary: Demand is accelerating due to higher net sales across key programs.

evidence.text: The organic sales increase of $0.6 billion in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022 was primarily due to higher net sales of $0.2 billion from our Air Power programs, $0.2 billion from our Advanced Technology programs, and $0.1 billion from our Land Warfare and Air Defense programs.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand with significant increases in net sales across key programs.

factors.summary: Backlog is growing, with significant new bookings.

evidence.text: Backlog was $35 billion at June 30, 2023 and $34 billion at December 31, 2022. In addition to a number of smaller bookings, in the quarter ended June 30, 2023, RMD booked $1.2 billion for AMRAAM for the U.S. Air Force and Navy and international customers, $294 million on a number of classified contracts, $265 million for Javelin for the U.S. Army and international customers, $251 million for AIM-9X Sidewinder short-range air-to-air missiles for the U.S. Navy and Air Force and international customers, and $237 million for CLEAVAR, an integrated U.S. Army Counter- Unmanned Aircraft Systems (C-UAS) defense system.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The evidence shows a growing backlog with substantial new bookings, indicating strong demand.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The unfavorable change in mix and other performance was primarily due to higher relative volume of early stage production programs within Land Warfare and Air Defense and Air Power.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While the revenue mix is shifting, the evidence suggests this shift may negatively impact margins, creating mixed signals.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is positive and accelerating.

evidence.text: The organic sales increase of $0.6 billion in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022 was primarily due to higher net sales of $0.2 billion from our Air Power programs, $0.2 billion from our Advanced Technology programs, and $0.1 billion from our Land Warfare and Air Defense programs.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary and evidence both show a clear increase in organic sales across multiple programs.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across key programs.

evidence.text: The increase in Advanced Technology programs includes higher net sales on certain classified programs awarded in 2022. The increase in Land Warfare and Air Defense programs was due to higher volumes across multiple programs.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Evidence indicates unit volumes are growing across key programs, signaling positive momentum.

factors.summary: The strongest end market momentum is in Air Power and Advanced Technology programs.

evidence.text: The increase in Air Power programs is primarily driven by higher net sales on the AMRAAM program driven by the award in the second quarter of 2023. The increase in Advanced Technology programs includes higher net sales on certain classified programs awarded in 2022.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The strongest end market momentum is in high-value programs with significant new sales and awards.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to mix changes.

evidence.text: The decrease in operating profit margins in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022, was primarily due to the change in mix and other performance discussed above.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are compressing due to unfavorable changes in product mix.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling investment confidence.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures in the six months ended June 30, 2023 increased by $128 million from the six months ended June 30, 2022 primarily due to investments in production facilities at Pratt & Whitney and Collins.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased capital expenditures signal confidence in future growth and investment in production facilities.

evidence.text: Total debt was $35,353 million at June 30, 2023 compared to $31,914 million at December 31, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Rising debt leverage indicates a potential financial risk.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing through dividends.

evidence.text: On June 5, 2023, the Board of Directors declared a dividend of $0.59 per share payable September 7, 2023 to shareowners of record at the close of business on August 18, 2023.
evidence.subsection: Factors Contributing to Total Change in Net Sales (dollars in millions) Organic (1) Acquisitions / Divestitures, net Other Total Change Net sales $ 633  $ (36) $ (11) $ 586

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased dividends signal positive returns for shareholders.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is deteriorating.

evidence.text: The $1.9 billion change in cash flows (used in) provided by operating activities from continuing operations in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to in the six months ended June 30, 2022, was primarily due to higher tax payments discussed below, a reduction in accounts payable and accrued liabilities primarily driven by timing of supplier and collaborator payments, higher accounts receivable as a result of increased sales volume and related factoring activity, partially offset by the timing of collaborator receivables, and the impact of the net change in contract assets and contract liabilities driven by the timing of collections, primarily at RMD.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Deteriorating free cash flow suggests financial headwinds.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by increased dividends.

evidence.text: On June 5, 2023, the Board of Directors declared a dividend of $0.59 per share payable September 7, 2023 to shareowners of record at the close of business on August 18, 2023.

factors.summary: New contract wins are a positive catalyst.

evidence.text: In addition to these bookings, in the six months ended June 30, 2023 RMD booked $1.2 billion to provide Patriot Air Defense system to Switzerland, $827 million on a number of classified contracts, $619 million on the SPY-6 Hardware Production and Sustainment contract for the U.S. Navy, $320 million on StormBreaker for the U.S. Air Force and Navy, $234 million on Naval Strike Missiles (NSM) for the U.S. Navy, and $212 million on Excalibur for the U.S. Army and international customers.

factors.summary: Government defense budgets are favorable, with spending directed toward core programs.

evidence.text: The increase in eliminations and other sales of $194 million in the six months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the six months ended June 30, 2022 was primarily due to an increase in intersegment eliminations, principally driven by RIS and Collins.
